title: ‘Totally unacceptable’: Trump rejects Iran’s ceasefire response sent via Pakistan
content: Iran has responded to a US ceasefire proposal through Pakistani mediators, seeking a permanent end to regional conflicts and guarantees for maritime security. President Trump rejected Tehran's position, calling it "TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!" amid rising tensions and drone incidents in the Gulf.

title: WFH, skip foreign trips, use metro: PM Modi's 9 tips to mitigate impact of global crisis
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged citizens to adopt lifestyle changes to conserve foreign exchange and mitigate the economic impact of global energy crises. Key suggestions include reducing fuel consumption by using public transport, carpooling, and opting for electric vehicles, alongside reviving work-from-home policies and limiting discretionary spending on gold and foreign travel.

title: IPL 2026: RCB win last-ball thriller to knock Mumbai Indians out
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a dramatic two-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians on the final ball, eliminating MI from IPL 2026. Krunal Pandya's resilient 73 and Bhuvneshwar Kumar's match-winning six off the last delivery propelled RCB to a thrilling chase of 167.

title: Plane carrying 22 UK citizens evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship lands in Manchester
content: Evacuees from the hantavirus-hit MV Hondius cruise ship have landed in the Netherlands, with passengers disembarking in good spirits. Authorities have begun quarantine procedures for the 26 individuals, including eight Dutch nationals, who arrived on a flight from Tenerife.  The ship experienced a hantavirus outbreak, leading to three passenger deaths.

title: Accenture CEO says companies are investing in AI, but adoption challenges remain
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet highlights a growing gap in Corporate America's AI integration, noting increased investment but lagging operational adoption.  The company is restructuring its operating model and workforce strategy, even tying employee promotions to AI proficiency, to accelerate AI-driven transformation. This proactive approach mirrors past digital shifts but is being implemented at a faster pace.

title: NTPC to approach Centre with feasibility report for its first N-power plant
content: NTPC is preparing to submit a feasibility report to the Centre for its first nuclear energy plant, aiming to contribute to India's goal of 100 GW atomic power capacity by 2047. The company has identified land in Bihar and plans to set up 30 GW of nuclear capacity across 14 states by 2047, with initial units of 700 MW each.

title: Asim Munir calls 2025 conflict 'battle of two ideologies'
content: Following India's strong warning, Pakistan's Army Chief declared the recent conflict a battle of ideologies, claiming victory. He asserted Pakistan's strategy was superior, framing the outcome as truth triumphing over falsehood. Meanwhile, a Pakistani minister's statement highlighting the Prime Minister and Army Chief's leadership drew public criticism, underscoring the military's significant influence.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn; gives 540 workers $240m
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Observatory that put Mount Everest in record books now lies in ruins
content: Bhuri Tori, a forgotten scientific outpost near Sironj, once served as the geodetic heart of undivided India. British surveyors used this ridge for the Great Trigonometrical Survey, fixing coordinates and calculating distances that helped measure Mount Everest. Today, this historically significant site lies neglected, with its structures vandalized and stones stolen.

title: Happy Mother's day: Top 100 wishes, messages, images, GIFs and greetings card
content: This Mother's Day, move beyond generic greetings to honor the women who raised you with meaningful messages. The article offers categorized heartfelt, witty, and minimalist quotes, acknowledging the "mental load" and celebrating "mothers of choice."

title: Boeing Honcho front-runner for NSIL chief post, could be first outsider
content: India's space commercial arm, NSIL, is poised for a significant shift as a private sector leader, reportedly from Boeing, is the front-runner for its CMD post. This marks a departure from its history of being led by Isro insiders. The selection process is in its final stages, aiming to bring external expertise to the burgeoning space PSU.

title: 92,000+ layoffs signal how AI era is driving job cuts and rise of 50-person unicorns
content: Over 92,000 tech jobs vanished in 2026, pushing the total since 2020 to nearly 900,000. Giants like Meta and Microsoft are cutting thousands, signaling a structural shift driven by AI efficiencies and pandemic over-hiring. Despite massive layoffs, tech titans are investing billions in AI, while startups achieve rapid growth with leaner teams, redefining business expansion in the AI era.

title: 'Please speed up my returns': Rahul's zinger for his 'BJP-supporter' financer wins internet
content: A mutual fund distributor, a long-time BJP supporter, shared how Rahul Gandhi maintained a professional relationship despite political differences. Gandhi humorously responded, urging faster investment returns. This light-hearted exchange garnered significant attention online, with many praising the civility and humor, while some questioned the professional ethics of revealing the client relationship.

title: 2 Indians safe after Hantavirus scare on cruise ship near Spain
content: Two Indian crew members aboard the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius, which anchored off Spain due to a hantavirus scare, are confirmed healthy and asymptomatic. Evacuated to the Netherlands for quarantine, their well-being is being closely monitored by Indian authorities in coordination with Spanish officials.  This incident highlights global health vigilance.

title: Railways staring at higher electricity costs after SC rules it qualifies as ‘consumer’
content: Indian Railways faces increased electricity costs following a Supreme Court ruling. The court declared the national transporter a "consumer" under the Electricity Act, making it liable for cross-subsidy and additional surcharges on open access power. This decision impacts the railways' operating ratio and power procurement expenses, overturning previous exemptions.

title: Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini drama takes wild turn with Aaron Rodgers
content: Aaron Rodgers and his mystery wife Brittani became unexpected talking points in the growing Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy after a viral social media post joked that no photos of Rodgers’ wife exist online. Fans flooded social media with conspiracy jokes about Brittani’s secrecy while Rodgers continues negotiating with the Pittsburgh Steelers ahead of the 2026 NFL season.

title: Megan Thee Stallion opens up on independence amid Klay Thompson drama
content: Megan Thee Stallion, the three time Grammy winner, and the Dallas Mavericks’ star player, Klay Thompson’s high profile break up has sparked shockwaves across the world as many are furious. As fans rooted for their future together, Megan Thee Stallion has now broken her silence on her life.

title: Khamzat Chimaev vs Sean Strickland UFC 328 purse: Expected payouts revealed
content: UFC 328 is set to deliver one of the biggest MMA cards of the year at the Prudential Center in New Jersey. The event features two title fights, several ranked contenders, and some of the UFC’s most popular fighters. Fans are especially focused on the middleweight championship clash. The fight between Khamzat Chimaev and Sean Strickland is headlining the pay-per-view card.

title: Non-binary Indian on student visa elected to Scottish Parliament
content: A non-binary transgender Indian national, Q Manivannan, has been elected as a Member of the Scottish Parliament (MSP) on a student visa. This historic win marks the first time a foreign citizen without permanent residency has held such a position. Manivannan's election has drawn controversy, with debates arising over their immigration status and political stance.

title: 'To restart Covid-era systems would be in national interest': PM Modi's stark appeal amid US-Iran war
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi called for careful use of petrol, diesel, and gas. He stated that reducing reliance on imported fuel will help India manage the ongoing war's effects. The Prime Minister also highlighted India's focus on energy security and investments in solar power. Several infrastructure projects worth around Rs 9,400 crore were inaugurated in Telangana.

title: Highway PPP projects opened to PE, pension and venture funds
content: Government has opened highway project bids to venture capital, private equity, infrastructure, and pension funds, a significant policy change. This move aims to attract substantial private investment by allowing these financially robust entities to partner with experienced construction firms.

title: Inside ‘mommy brain’: How pregnancy and childbirth rewire the maternal mind
content: Pregnancy and childbirth trigger significant physiological, psychological, and emotional transformations in women, often leading to "mommy brain" characterized by forgetfulness and mental fog. Scientific studies reveal neural recalibration, not decline, as the brain adapts to caregiving demands. This experience, while common, requires understanding and support for new mothers navigating identity shifts and cognitive loads.

title: Golden duck! Virat Kohli equals unwanted IPL milestone against MI
content: Virat Kohli's IPL 2026 campaign hit a new low with a golden duck against Mumbai Indians, his second consecutive failure. Deepak Chahar claimed the crucial wicket, adding to Kohli's struggles against MI. Earlier, Mumbai Indians posted 166/7 despite a poor start, thanks to Tilak Varma's fifty and Naman Dhir's contribution.

title: '168 kids that your boss killed ... ': Iran 'congratulates' White House press secy over baby news
content: Iran congratulated White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt on her newborn daughter while referencing the deadly US-backed strikes on an Iranian school in Minab. The Iranian Embassy in Armenia urged Leavitt to "think of the mothers" of the 168 children killed in the attack, which occurred during Operation Epic Fury.

title: Accountsmaxxing? Decoding Gen Z's chaotic yet genius approach to finance
content: Gen Z is navigating a complex financial landscape, balancing early earning and investing with impulsive spending and trend-driven bets. While digitally savvy and embracing new tools, they often overlook essential safety nets like insurance. Experts urge a shift from quick gains to disciplined, long-term wealth building, splitting finances strategically for stability.

title: How US-Iran war is making life more expensive & difficult for you
content: A distant Middle East conflict is now impacting Indian households, driving up costs for everyday essentials like LPG, cooking oil, and dry fruits. Furniture, electronics, and even paint are becoming pricier due to rising petrochemical and shipping expenses. Air travel and fuel prices are also climbing, with potential for further hikes as oil companies face significant losses.

title: ‘Fuel price hike inevitable’: State-run oil firms lose over Rs 1 lakh cr in 10 weeks
content: State-owned oil companies are facing over Rs 1 lakh crore in losses due to soaring global fuel prices, with daily under-recoveries reaching Rs 1,700 crore. Despite this, petrol and diesel prices remain unchanged, impacting the financial health of these crucial energy providers. A fuel price hike now appears inevitable, with the government facing a political decision on timing and extent.

title: TCS Nashik case: 'Planned bid to trouble educated Muslim youths,' says Owaisi
content: AIMIM chief Asaduddin Owaisi alleged the Nashik TCS exploitation case is an intentional attempt to target educated Muslim youths, claiming it lacks legal standing and faces a media trial. He stated the corporator involved will contest the matter legally, asserting the party's unawareness of the incident and that recovered items are not illegal.

title: 'PM seems to have memory loss': Cong lashes out amid Kerala CM suspense
content: Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala criticized Prime Minister Modi for his remarks on the Kerala CM selection delay, pointing out the BJP's own 50-day wait in Delhi. He emphasized the Congress's democratic process versus the BJP's 'authoritarian' approach. Consultations are ongoing, with a decision expected soon after discussions with leaders and MLAs.

title: 'Play Tamil song before Vande Mataram': Row erupts over TN CM Vijay's swearing in event
content: CPI state secretary M Veerapandiyan has objected to the order of songs played at Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's swearing-in ceremony, where 'Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu' was placed third after 'Vande Mataram' and the national anthem. He stated this violates long-standing convention and demanded a public explanation for the lapse, urging the government to restore the state song's primacy.

title: Urvil Patel scripts history, smashes joint-fastest fifty in IPL
content: Urvil Patel unleashed an explosive batting display, smashing 25 runs off Avesh Khan in the fifth over and another 25 off Digvesh Rathi in the sixth. He became the first batter in IPL history to hit six sixes in his first eight balls, racing to 41. Patel then achieved a breathtaking half-century in just 13 balls, equalling the IPL record.

title: 'Don’t begin by claiming govt is broke': Ex-CM Stalin's message to newly elected Vijay
content: Tamil Nadu's new Chief Minister Vijay claimed the previous government left a Rs 10 lakh crore debt and an empty treasury. However, former CM M.K. Stalin refuted this, stating the state's debt is within limits and urging Vijay not to start his tenure with such claims. Vijay vowed transparency and prioritizing public welfare.

title: YouTuber Anurag Dwivedi’s lavish wedding party that invited ED to a dark underbelly
content: A popular YouTuber's lavish lifestyle, including a Dubai cruise wedding, has drawn the Enforcement Directorate's attention. Investigators allege his empire was built on illegal betting promotions and money laundering. The case highlights the blurred lines between fantasy sports and betting, and the regulatory gaps exploited by online platforms.

title: North Korea prepares nuclear dead man's switch; what prompted dangerous move?
content: North Korea has amended its constitution, mandating an automatic nuclear strike if leader Kim Jong-un is assassinated or incapacitated by foreign adversaries. This revision, prompted by concerns over recent decapitation attacks in Iran, formalizes retaliatory procedures. The move underscores Pyongyang's heightened security fears amid evolving geopolitical threats.

title: Oil slick near Kharg Island? Iran's crude industry at breaking point amid US blockade
content: An oil spill near Iran's Kharg Island, the country's primary oil export hub, has raised concerns amid a US naval blockade. Satellite images show a significant oil slick, potentially amounting to 3,000 barrels lost. This incident occurs as Iran faces a storage crisis due to the blockade, with capacity nearing its limit.

title: Who is Urvil Patel? CSK batter who smashed joint-fastest IPL fifty in 13 balls
content: Urvil Patel etched his name in IPL history by smashing a 13-ball half-century for Chennai Super Kings against Lucknow Super Giants. This explosive innings equalled Yashasvi Jaiswal's record for the fastest fifty in the tournament. Patel, a 27-year-old wicketkeeper-batter from Gujarat, has a proven track record of rapid scoring in domestic cricket.

title: 'Yeh lo ... ': New dad Suryakumar Yadav distributes sweets before RCB clash
content: Suryakumar Yadav celebrated the birth of his daughter by distributing sweets to the paparazzi at Mumbai airport ahead of the IPL 2026 clash against RCB. He will lead Mumbai Indians in the absence of Hardik Pandya, whose fitness is being monitored. The team faces a crucial match to boost playoff hopes.

title: Suvendu Adhikari to chair first Cabinet meeting on May 11
content: On Monday, Suvendu Adhikari will enter Nabanna for the first time as the Chief Minister; at his first cabinet meeting, portfolios will be allocated to five Ministers

title: Dravidian parties got trapped in vicious cycle of cash-for-votes and corruption, says T.N. Minister Arunraj
content: “The Dravidian parties played a role in making T.N. a social justice State. But their initial idealism became diluted. They got trapped in a narrow path of corruption-ridden governance and family politics to such an extent that they couldn’t come out of it,” says the TVK leader

title: PM visit turns city into high security zone as crowds throng Parade Grounds
content: Tight security, massive mobilisation and political spectacle mark Modi’s city visit for multiple programmes, culminating in a public meeting at Parade Grounds attended by over 75,000 people

title: Maritime safety and security is of primordial importance to Indian Ocean Region: Secretary-General
content: Any disruption in the Indian Ocean will have a deep impact on the economy across the region, says Sanjiv Ranjan, Secretary-General of the Indian Ocean Rim Association; war in West Asia overshadows India’s agenda for IORA summit next year, marking 30 years of the grouping

title: British-era Lakshmanaswami Town Hall restored in Vellore
content: The government hall once housed the district museum of North Arcot region when it was established in 1985 by the Department of Education

title: No intent to insult, says Apu Joseph amid KC(M) protest
content: Kerala Congress leader and MLA-elect says his statement about welcoming Kerala Congress (M) with open arms was driven by a genuine wish that K.M. Mani’s party should not fade away

title: In Kerala, nearly half of Congress’ MLAs-elect are young faces
content: Congress decision to field maximum number of youth and student activists brought rich dividends as the young candidates connected well with Gen Z and young voters and won the popular mandate, feels one MLA-elect

title: CPI Kerala State secretary Binoy Viswam criticises personality cults, reiterates CPI claim to Deputy Opposition Leader’s post
content: After the CPI national council meeting in New Delhi, Viswam describes the “unexpected” LDF defeat as a warning issued by the people

title: Congress claims Instagram blocked Rahul Gandhi's reel and photo post featuring Vijay due to MeitY rules
content: Sources in the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology clarified that it had nothing to do with the action and it was because the platform’s internal system mistakenly flagged the post for blocking

title: Tradition of singing Tamil Thaai Vazhthu at beginning, National Anthem at end will continue, says Minister Aadhav Arjuna
content: In Tamil Nadu, the rendering of Tamil Thai Vazhthu as the third song is not acceptable to the Tamil Nadu Government led by the TVK, he said

title: AAP holds protests against BJP over ED action against Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora
content: The AAP leaders and volunteers burnt effigies of the BJP-led Centre and raised slogans against it. They were also carrying placards which read 'ED-BJP gathjod murdabad'

title: Subsidy for power supply to domestic consumers to cross ₹10,000-crore mark
content: The TVK government’s announcement of 200 units of free power for domestic consumers using below 500 units bimonthly will further increase the State’s annual subsidy burden by nearly ₹2,000 crore

title: ED gets seven-day custody of Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora
content: Mr. Arora was arrested on May 9 at his official residence in Chandigarh by the Enforcement Directorate in an alleged money-laundering case.

title: Move to secure technical sanction for STP in West Hill sparks protest
content: District Collector issues the direction at a recent meeting attended by officials from the Kozhikode Corporation, District Suchitwa Mission, State Pollution Control Board, and Local Self-Government department to initiate steps to obtain technical sanction for the project

title: India-Trinidad archival pact to help diaspora trace ancestral roots: Jaishankar
content: Mr. Jaishankar said Prime Minister Narendra Modi attached high importance to creating a database of the Girmitya community and conducting research on its legacy

title: Governor’s role in government formation | Explained
content: What does Article 164(1) say about the appointment of the Chief Minister? When does the Governor use discretionary powers? Which commissions recommended guidelines for government formation in a hung Assembly? Why has the conduct of Governors in hung Assemblies raised concerns?

title: Man held on charge of attacking another with hammer in Pudukad in Thrissur
content: Cyril of  Potta was taken into custody for attacking Deenas Davis, a native of Marathakkara; victim critical after head injuries

title: Tripura pharmacists to join strike on May 20
content: The All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists has three major demands — stop online sale of medicines; stop deep discounts from corporate-run industry; and stop the sale of spurious medicines

title: PIL in SC for reviewing wages of priests, sevadars and temple staff in govt.-controlled temples
content: “Priests and temple staff are not getting even the minimum wage prescribed by the State for unskilled and semi-skilled workers. This is a systemic exploitation,” the plea said.

title: I-PAC emerges as Trinamool’s fall guy after West Bengal Assembly poll debacle
content: Working for Trinamool Congress since 2021, political consultancy firm I-PAC has been instrumental in several past electoral victories for party; after 2026 Assembly poll debacle, a section of party leaders are blaming I-PAC for defeat

title: PM Modi urges citizens to cut fuel use, avoid foreign travel
content: Mr. Modi suggests reviving some of the practices adopted during the COVID-19 pandemic, including work from home arrangements, online conferences and virtual meetings.

title: Mamata created false narrative on BJP restricting consumption of fish: Dilip Ghosh
content: Mr. Ghosh also said fish and rice are integrated with the culinary habits of Bengal, and assured people that the BJP Government would continue to promote the state’s food culture and traditions

title: Solicitor General launches two tomes on humour in court
content: The CJI says the books are replete with hilarious anecdotes and incidents Tushar Mehta has collected after raiding law libraries, sifting through old case files and digging out obscure trial records

title: Karnataka is No 1 in revenue, panchayat services, claims Priyank Kharge
content: The Minister was launching a large-scale distribution drive for revenue department benefits at Dr. S.M. Pandit Rangamandir in Kalaburagi

title: From flight journey to land gifts, SSC toppers receive unique rewards in A.P.
content: In a heartwarming gesture from Vizianagaram district, A. Hemanth, a student of ZPHS at Cheepurupalli, who secured 581 marks in the SSC examinations, was given his first-ever flight journey experience from Visakhapatnam to Vijayawada

title: Opposition leaders caution against granting L-G enhanced powers over telecom in J&K
content: J&K Congress chief says power over Internet shutdowns, interception, blocking of communication networks, and decryption authority is ‘yet another dent on the democratic governance mechanism of the elected government’

title: ED probing alleged scams worth nearly ₹4,000 crore across the education sector
content: The latest case involves entities linked to Al-Falah Group, in which the agency recently re-arrested Group chief Jawad Ahmad Siddiqui for his alleged role in the use of forged papers to acquire land in Delhi.

title: A.P. EAPCET-2026 to begin from May 12 across A.P. and Telangana
content: A total of 3,55,011 candidates have applied for the examination through the online mode this year, says APSCHE Secretary

title: Uttar Pradesh Cabinet expanded with six new Ministers
content: As the BJP eyes caste balance ahead of Assembly election early next year, Bhupendra Singh Chaudhary and Manoj Kumar Pandey take oath as Cabinet Ministers

title: Tamil Nadu government formation updates: TN Assembly to convene first session; Speaker election on May 12
content: Former T.N. Chief Minister Stalin reacts sharply to Mr. Vijay's remarks that DMK government had left State treasury empty with debt burden of ₹10 lakh crore

title: Dissent brews in CPI(M) as two dozen members quit in Palakkad
content: Move protesting against the demotion of an area committee member who had criticised the party’s district secretary

title: No Pink Saheli Card, No Free Bus Ride? Delhi Eyes Mandatory Use From July
content: The move is part of a broader plan to integrate the scheme with the National Common Mobility Card (NCMC) platform under the Centre's "One Nation, One Card" initiative.

title: Vijay Oath Ceremony Highlights: "I'm Like Your Son, Your Brother," Vijay Tells Supporters After Taking Oath
content: Vijay Oath Ceremony LIVE Updates: Vijay, the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief, now has the support of 120 MLAs.

title: Iran Responds To US Peace Plan, As Fresh Drone Attacks Rattle Ceasefire
content: Netanyahu said on Sunday that Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium must be removed before the US-Israeli war against Iran could be considered finished.

title: Trump Says US Could Hit 'Every Target' In Iran In 2 Weeks
content: The comments come as Iran is reported to have responded to the latest US proposal on ending a conflict that began on February 28 with US and Israeli strikes on Iran.

title: Unseen Since March, Mojtaba Khamenei Meets Iran Military Head
content: Khamenei issued "new directives and guidance for the continuation of operations to confront the enemy", state TV said.

title: Kerala Standoff: Rahul Gandhi Caught Between KC Venugopal And Satheesan
content: While KC Venugopal is reportedly the choice of the MLAs, VD Satheesan appears to be the choice of the public. Congress workers in Kerala have been taking to the streets to hold rallies in support of Satheesan

title: Delhi's Rs 3.5 Crore Scheme To Support Children Leaving Care Homes After 18
content: The Delhi government on Sunday approved a Rs 3.5-crore aftercare scheme for youths leaving child care institutions after turning 18, offering support for higher education, skill training, jobs and financial assistance to help them transition to independent living.

title: 'Bengal In Deep Pit, BJP To Bring Change': PM Modi In Gujarat
content: The Prime Minister expressed his gratitude for the "increasing blessings" of the electorate, noting that the BJP has now secured the loyalty of four consecutive generations in Gujarat.

title: PM Modi's 'Stabs Own Leaders In Back' Jibe At Congress Over Kerala Indecision
content: The Mallikarjun Kharge-led party hasn't yet announced the name of their chief ministerial pick in Kerala, several days after its alliance scored a landslide victory in the Assembly elections.

title: 'London Tourist', 95,000 Pounds Promise: Gwalior Man Duped Of Rs 1.23 Lakh
content: The woman allegedly claimed she lived in London and planned to visit India carrying 95,000 British pounds, worth around Rs 1.22 crore.

title: Adani Group To Create 1,20,000 New Jobs In Madhya Pradesh By 2030: Pranav Adani
content: Adani Enterprises Director Pranav Adani said on Sunday that the Adani Group is poised to create 120,000 new jobs In Madhya Pradesh by 2030 across various sectors ranging from cement and mining to power.

title: PM Modi's Gati-Shakti Reforms Make Madhya Pradesh Key Investment Hub: Pranav Adani
content: Addressing the Bhoomi Pujan ceremony of Adani Cement in Guna, Pranav Adani said the project marks not just the beginning of a cement plant, but a new chapter in Madhya Pradesh's development journey.

title: DMK Elects Udhayanidhi Stalin As Legislature Party Leader
content: The DMK has elected former Deputy Chief Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin as the legislature party leader in the House.

title: Woman Killed In Tiger Attack In Maharashtra, Second Big Cat Attack In 2 Days
content: A 65-year-old woman was killed in a tiger attack in Maharashtra's Gondia district when she had gone to collect tendu leaves on Sunday morning, a forest department official said.

title: 'Grabbed Its Neck': 13-Year-Old Boy's Daring Face-Off With Leopard
content: Satyam Thakur, a Class 8 student, was playing near a farm well close to his home when a leopard emerged from nearby bushes and attacked him.

title: Iraq Never Knew Its Desert Was Hiding An Israeli Military Base
content: The installation housed Israeli special forces and functioned as a logistical hub for the Israeli air force.

title: Mocked Over Body Donation, Madhya Pradesh Man Plans Own Death Ceremony
content: Ramlotan Kushwaha, a resident of Atrabediya village in Uchehra, has printed condolence-style invitation cards for the ceremonies.

content: At TOI Sports Desk, reporters work around the clock to bring you comprehensive updates from the world of sports.
